questions.question: Describe the concept of 'Sequence Points' in an Abstract Syntax Tree (AST) for a compiler design. Give an example of how a compiler handles sequence points in a simple arithmetic expression, e.g., 2 + 3 * 4. (5 marks)
questions.hint: Sequence points refer to the positions in the source code where the compiler can safely assume that certain operations have been completed.

questions.question: Explain the difference between a 'Three Address Code' (TAC) representation and its corresponding 'Symbolic Representation'. Use an example to illustrate your point. (8 marks)
questions.hint: Three Address Code is a representation of an instruction where each instruction has only three operands (source, destination, and operand), whereas the symbolic representation is a more human-readable form of the TAC.

questions.question: Describe the process of 'Type Inference' in a compiler design and provide an example of how it would work for a simple variable declaration, e.g., int x; (10 marks)
questions.hint: Type inference involves the compiler deducing the type of a variable based on the context in which it is used. In this example, the compiler would infer that 'x' is of type 'int' because it is declared with the 'int' keyword.
